title: bassia parviflora var. costata
intro: Bassia parviflora var. costata, also known as Chinese silk vine or paraffin weed, is a type of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and bronchitis, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, Bassia parviflora var. costata is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in the form of dried flowers and roots.
